latest.title: Appointment of Joint Corporate Broker
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8558S
Luceco PLC
13 February 2026
13 February 2026
LUCECO PLC
Appointment of Joint Corporate Broker
Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group"), the leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems, announces the appointment of Jefferies International Limited ("Jefferies") as joint corporate broker with immediate effect. Jefferies will act alongside the Company's existing corporate broker, Peel Hunt.
Luceco plc
Contact
John Hornby, Chief Executive Officer
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer
Jefferies International Limited
Contact
Thierry Le Palud
020 7029 8000
Philip Noblet
Sam Barnett
Sodali & Co
Contact
James White
078 5543 2699 / 079 3535 1934
Pete Lambie
Tilly Abraham
Note to Editors
Luceco plc (LSE:LUCE) is a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems. The Group designs and manufactures its market-leading range of wiring accessories, EV chargers, LED lighting, and portable power products at its state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, distributing them through professional, wholesale and retail channels.
For more information, please visit
www.lucecoplc.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: LUCE.L is a UK‑listed technology company that designs, develops and sells high‑performance optical fibre solutions, including cables, connectors and splicing equipment for telecom and data‑centre networks.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Optical fibre is the backbone of modern high‑speed internet and data traffic. LUCE’s niche products target the growing demand for ultra‑high bandwidth connectivity, giving it a foothold against larger incumbents while serving a rapidly expanding market.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has been navigating a challenging revenue environment, reflected in recent earnings updates and board changes. It remains cash‑positive but has seen a significant drop in share price, trading at a steep discount to its historical valuation.

rns_narrative: The company has been making governance moves—appointing a new non‑executive director and a joint corporate broker—suggesting a focus on improving oversight and market access.
Financial reporting has been consistent, with several full‑year and interim results released. The latest updates show a decline in earnings but also indicate some operational stabilization.
Shareholder activity is evident, with directors and other stakeholders disclosing holdings, which points to an ongoing attempt to align interests and potentially attract additional capital.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Investors are hopeful that an upcoming product launch and improved supply‑chain logistics will spark a rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics worry that continued revenue pressure and global supply‑chain disruptions could keep the stock under pressure.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from neutral to cautiously bullish, with a few bullish voices pointing to the company’s resilience.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, giving the setup a high surprise potential if any catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: A product launch or major order win announced in the next quarter could lift demand.
Positive earnings surprise from the upcoming Q3 report would validate the company’s operational turnaround.
A volume spike that breaks the 52‑week low by a significant margin would signal renewed investor interest.
Any new partnership or regulatory approval that expands the company’s customer base would be a strong catalyst.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear breakout above the recent low, a sustained 2‑week volume increase, or an earnings beat that exceeds market expectations.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery after a deep capitulation. The high APEX score indicates a tight price range, so a breakout could be imminent if the catalysts above play out.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is a deep crash bottom (70‑95% down) with a high probability of a mean‑reversion bounce. Historical RED‑color signals deliver an average upside of about 160% when the price recovers to the midpoint of its recent decline. If the share climbs to that level, the upside would be roughly 2:1 relative to the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The risk of further decline exists if supply‑chain issues persist and the company fails to generate a revenue turnaround. A worst‑case scenario would see the price stay near the bottom of its current range, leaving a limited upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped near the current trough, while upside could approach 160% if the mean‑reversion plays out. The asymmetry is roughly 2:1, favouring a contrarian long stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 52‑week low with volume at least twice the average daily volume. Allocate 10% of your discretionary portfolio, set a stop 10% below the entry, and trail it as the price moves higher.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the next earnings release or a confirmed product launch announcement. If either triggers a volume surge and a clear price breakout, consider a position.
playbook.if_passing: No genuine terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy have surfaced, so the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash has left LUCE.L at a steep discount; if a product launch or earnings beat materialises, the upside could be around 160%, making a contrarian long a compelling play.
